summary:
The case involves a 3-month-old infant presenting with a brief resolved unexplained event (BRUE), which includes episodes of cyanosis and muscle hypotonia resolving within two minutes. Matching was employed in diagnostic studies involving this population to control for potential confounders like age, socioeconomic status, and familial health history when comparing outcomes among children who experienced versus did not experience BRUE events.
